titlestmt.author: Compiled by B. Shepherd, K. Jacklin

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-08-09 00:28:10 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence; accounts; contracts; leases; surveys; bonds; deeds; receipts; other land, legal, and business papers; political broadsides; pamphlets; editorials; handbills; and speeches.
did.abstract: Papers pertaining to the early history of Jefferson County, New York.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Elisha Camp was postmaster, militia officer, lawyer, deputy New York State district attorney, politician, newspaper owner and editor, banker, landowner, and land agent in Sackets Harbor, New York. His sons became military officers and continued in the family businesses in Sackets Harbor.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection contains correspondence, accounts, contracts, leases, surveys, bonds, deeds, receipts, and other land, legal, and business papers, political broadsides and pamphlets pertaining to the early history of Jefferson County, New York, particularly on the township of Hounsfield, on the transfer of land from intermediate investors to actual settlers, on the attempts to make Sackets Harbor a great commercial center, and on the accompanying economic, political, and social developments. Collection is chiefly concerned with Camp, who served as representative of his brother-in-law Augustus Sacket, and for other absentee landowners such as David A. Ogden, Thomas L. Ogden, Peter Kemble, Ezra Hounsfield, Lemuel Storrs, Henry Champion, Herman LeRoy, William Bayard, James M. Ellis, Cornelius Ray, Michael Hogan, James Le Ray Chaumont, the executors of the Nicholas Olive estate and the executors of Nicholas Gouvernor. Papers after 1845 are chiefly family letters between Camp and his children and Elisha E. Camp who was in Mexico (1847), Green Bay, Wisconsin, Washington Territory, and Oregon, and a series of Civil War letters. Names of persons and firms represented include David Boyd, President James Buchanan, DeWitt Clinton, James C. Duane, George Hale, L. Seymour, Gerrit Smith, Jesse C. Smith, Charles Smyth, Joseph C. Yates, the Jefferson County Agricultural Society, Jefferson County Bank, Lake Ontario Steamboat Co., Ogden Land Co., and the Sackets Harbor and Ellisburgh Railroad.

Other topics include the Sackets Harbor and Saratoga Railroad, Sackets Harbor Steam and Ferry Boat Co., Union Academy, and the Watertown and Rome Railroad. Also, handbills, editorials, and speeches concerning National Republican Party politics on the New York State and local levels, and containing uncomplimentary references to the Bucktail faction. Other papers pertain to the militia system, the United States Navy, restrictive fees on Lake Ontario commerce, and water rights at Brownville, New York.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Elisha Camp papers, #696. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection, including additional materials donated in 2020, were completely re-processed in 2024.

controlaccess.geogname: United States -- History -- Civil War, 1861-1865 -- Personal narratives.
controlaccess.subject: Manufactures -- New York (State) -- Jefferson County.
Shipping -- New York (State) -- Jefferson County.
Politics, Practical -- New York (State) -- Jefferson County.
Mills and mill-work -- New York (State) -- Jefferson County.
Land titles -- Exchange of.
Land titles -- Valuation.
Inland water transportation -- New York (State) -- Jefferson County.
Mormon Church -- History.
controlaccess.corpname: Jefferson County Agricultural Society (Jefferson County, N.Y.)
Sackets Harbor Steam and Ferry Boat Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to William Waning describing Mill House Lot.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to William Waning describing Mill House Lot.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letter from Ogden to Camp concerning sale of Sacket's estate which they purchased.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Nathan Camp regarding Pennsylvania lands; David Ogden setting forth directions for renting land, laying out a street, and cutting fuel; W. Brown directing how to dispose of money Brown won in a bet; Le Ray de Chaumont agreeing to a commission for sale of 41 lots of land; Peter Kemble and Ezra Hounsfield giving instructions for supervising lands; T.L. Ogden concerning lands in Hounsfield.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Thomas Ogden regarding sale of lots.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Lemuel Stores regarding cutting of lumber and selling of land and hay; Thomas Ogden concerning sale and supervision of land; and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Thomas Ogden.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes buying shares of stock in the Lake Ontario Boat Company for General Jacob Brown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note buying shares of stock in the Lake Ontario Boat Company for General Jacob Brown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Butterfield and Kendall concerning steamboat; Augustus Sacket describing financial troubles; James Paul Allaire letters describing ship design drafts and measurements, engines, list of machinery purchased.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter addressed to "gentleman of the association" from an anonymous writer called "modesty," describing undesirable traits of the human species and, namely, the men and women of the association. Also includes a letter requiring all officers, non-commissioned officers, and musicians to train, as well as other military-related letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding resignations and discharges from the 21st Regiment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding Regiment activities.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter regarding land purchased in Massachusetts from the Seneca in 1826; letter from Colonel Henry Leavenworth regarding building a canal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Gerrit Smith declining to sell land for the Union Academy, and a letter from Elisha's nephew, E.E. Camp, regarding his campaigns against the Free Masons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter Leonard Dennison regarding convention and campaigning for Harrison for president.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter Leonard Dennison regarding convention and campaigning for Harrison for president, written on a promotional pamphlet for the Albany Convention; letter concerning the outcome of the 1836 election.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter of complaint regarding Camp's canal running throuhg the swamp land.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters referencing Camp's paper and lumber mill burning down.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter (February 12) from John May regarding politics in Oswego, Pirates at Fort Henry, and the British Empire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Hiram Barney about the Young Men's Total Abstinence Society and temperance; Thomas Chittenden writing about appropriations necessary for expansion and local government/conflicts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from a "looker on" criticising Camp's speech in Watertown; a letter from Gerrit Smith regarding the Temperance Movement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Elisha Ely to his parents describing the relationship with the Indigenous Peoples of the Washington Territories, and writes of how they describe their treatment by "white men."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Elisha Ely describing the collaboration between the Mormons and the local tribes against the United States government (the Utah War), and another describing the activities of the local (unnamed) tribe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Erskine describing his regiment movements near Fredericksburg.

did.unittitle: Philo Ellinwood correspondence and documents to Elisha Camp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In original grouping.

did.unittitle: Thomas L. Ogden correspondence and documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In original grouping.

did.unittitle: Certificate appointing Camp as Deputy District Attorney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Nathan Sanford, New York District Attorney. Includes letter from Sanford.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIa. General family documents and correspondence
Boundaries of land left to Elisha Camp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Land left to Camp in his father's will. Bounded by land belonging to Amos Northrop, Anabel Stone, Israel Baldwin, Epemetus Gunn, David Northrop, Widow Power. Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Heziah Camp account with William Tracy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account for boarding her sons Elisha and Martin and for Elisha's schooling.

did.unittitle: Passport from the Kingdom of Wurttemburg
Drawings of a mill or other equipment
Harriet Hale Camp American Missionary Society certificates
Emma E. Camp American Tract Society membership certificate
Subseries IIb. Elisha Ely Camp documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters discussing leaving on a whaling vessel, from E. Denison and James P. Lee; letters from Elisha Camp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some letters include passing mentions of the Mexican-American war andmilitary orders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some letters include passing mentions of the Mexican-American war. Includes "A Mexican Song," written in spanish by Elisha.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a promissory note co-signed by Ulysses S. Grant; several letters from Erskine Camp describing life in New Orleans, railroads, the levees, and the 1852 election.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter describing the death of Lieutenant Ed Russell after he captured several Indigenous peoples; a letter J.R. Bergan (September 24) describing two "Indian Wars", one of which ended with a small pox outbreak and the second had yet to start in Rogue River Valley. A few letters mention "Captain Grant" at Vancouver Barracks, referring to Ulysses S. Grant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter regarding the American Guano Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several letters from Elisha Camp discuss the general political and military state of affairs. Includes a letter from Lieutenant Joshua Spencer to William L. Marcy recommending Elisha for a position in the Navy; a letter with an excerpt from a speech by Francis Miles Finch on the occasion of the execution of Nathan Hale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several letters mention: the Utah War; the Indian Wars, including mention of Colonel Steptoe and Coeur d'Alene (May 29th); dances and basket weaving of indigenous peoples of Yukon (Jan 30th).

did.unittitle: Notes and quotes on various topics
Subseries IIc. Edgar B. Camp documents
Subseries IId. Erskine M. Camp documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes military appointment letters and special orders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes many letters with Mary Alice Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invoice of Ordnance and Ordnance Stores for Camp's regiment in Texas, as well as monthly inventory of supplies.

did.unittitle: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions certificate
Series III. Hale, McCrea, Smith, and Clarke Family documents
Letter from Jabez Deck to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Providence, Rhode Island. Directions for playing a card game.

did.unittitle: Letter from Elisha Hale, Glastonbury, to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elisha Hale writes to his brother George concerning shoes and rum sent to George by Captain Chase's vessel, also produce, building, livestock, and scarcity of money.

did.unittitle: Letter from John Hirwell, Boston, to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns receipt of playing cards and possible purchase of a spinet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Receipt for cutting ninety packs of geographical cards.

did.unittitle: Letter from Boswell Welles to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns social life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Receipt for rum, partially cancelling debt of Jabez Peck to Hale's brother Samuel.

did.unittitle: Receipt to M. Wheeler for printing cards
Letter from Elisha Hale, Glastonbury, to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns sale of livestock, produce, West India goods, and family affairs.

did.unittitle: Letter from Elisha Hale, Glastonbury, to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns shipping pork via Captain Miller, and giving advice for being a successful merchant.

did.unittitle: Letter from Elisha Hale, Glastonbury, to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns shipping pork, purchasing sugar, tea, and a new partner in the trading hourse of Brown and Benson.

did.unittitle: Letter from Asher Robbins to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns the death of Joanna.

did.unittitle: Letter from Jack Taylor to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns daily doings, Lucy's marriage, and William Mosley.

did.unittitle: Letter from Elisha Hale, Glastonbury, to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns merchant ttrading of cloth, fish, and salt.

did.unittitle: Letter from George and Ashbel Welles to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter of introduction recommending Sam Welles, about to enter the mercantile business.

did.unittitle: Clothing account with Brown and Benson
Letter from Abner Moseley to George Hale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns friendship and love. Quote from Shenston.

did.unittitle: Letter from Abner Moseley to George Hale
Certificate appointing Hale as Lieutenant Colonel
Legal document with seal transferring the position of Sheriff from George Hale to Peter Adams
Certificate with seal appointing Hale as Commissioner of Taxes in the county of Ulster
Subseries IIIb. McCrea and Tschappat families
Photographs of Tully McCrea and family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual review inspection concerning procedure, dress, etc. Signed by L. Bauman

did.unittitle: Draft of committee report on Lieutenant Cross's "unwarranted behavior."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report describes Cross infringing "on our rights as Citizens by a Public Officer." Cross backtracks and the committee recommends suspension of hitherto proposed measures.

did.unittitle: First enlistment and constitution for Sackets Harbor Artillery Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Warrant for the appearance of 16 men equipped for militiary duty from Captain Elisha Camp.

did.unittitle: Roll of the officers, non-commissioned officers, and band of the 21st Regiment Artillery
List of men in the 21st Regiment Artillery and amount paid for their services
Receipt roll of militia commanded by Christian Blystone
Convention of Military Officers, Utica, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In original grouping.

did.unittitle: Blank State of New York Militia certificate
Resolution of Thanks to the citizens of Sackets Harbor from the Ninth US Infantry
Subseries Va. Land Surveys and Maps
Paul Hutchins sells to Elisha Camp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hutchins, heir of Ebenezer Hutchins of Pittsfield Massachusetts, sells to Camp the title to gratuity or bounty lands in Hannibal, Brutus, and Romulus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Survey of two lots of land: one next to land belonging to Garlick and Clark, the second next to land belonging to T. Camp. Surveyor Hephia Foot [?].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of field notes by William Shephard on survey of Samuel Franklin tract of land, and John Mitchels survey of Mill Seat.

did.unittitle: Notebook of land surveys with maps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes surveys in Hounsfield belonging to Benjamin West, E. Tell's land, Sherwin's lot (with map), letter to Augustus Sacket from Egbert Ten Eyck with survey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes survey by Aaron Blodget of lots of JF Mevain and Powers; survey by Blodget of Simon Reed's lot.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes survey of Samuel Wilder's land in Hounsfield.

did.unittitle: Map of the south half of the town of Hounsfield
Colored map of Sackets Harbor lots
Plan of proposed division of Sackets Harbor
Subseries Vb. Sackets Harbor business documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original groupings

did.unittitle: Union Pacific Railroad Co. reports and account statements
State Bank Sackets Harbor and Elisha Camp
Three dollar note from Mohawk Bank
List of passengers traveling by boat
Documents pertaining to Steam Boat Sophia, schooner, and canals
Subseries Vc. Bills, accounts, and receipts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes accounts and receipts with general post office, bill of costs, and receipt for wheat.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes accounts and receipts with general post office, expenditures for July Fourth celebration, bill of costs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes accounting from the estate of George Hale signed by Elisha Camp and Thomas Hale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes list of names of laborers and number of days worked.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Augustus Sacket accounts with Elisha Camp; other accounts settled.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes list of machinery and one of items purchased for the Steamboat Sophia; list of passengers and freight from Sackets Harbor to Kingston.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes accounts with Allaire for ship; Steamboat Sophia accounts, bills, and receipts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes accounts with Allaire for ship; Steamboat Sophia accounts, bills, and receipts; lumber mill inventory.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes accounts with Allaire for ship; Steamboat Sophia accounts, bills, and receipts; lumber mill inventory.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Steamboat Sophia accounts, bills, and receipts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Steamboat Sophia accounts, bills, and receipts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Steamboat Sophia accounts, bills, and receipts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes lumber mill accounts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes E. Camp's work book, with names of workers and accounts; shares to Hounsfield Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Grist Mill account book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes surplus water taxes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes booklets of stone quarry accounts and lumber/log statistics (1834-1837).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account book with some pages covered in clippings, as well as pressed leaves.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account book from 1807, with newspaper images pasted in in 1873.

did.unittitle: Business Letter Book - signed by Camp as cashier; seems to be banking

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Court of Common Pleas regarding division of Henry Leavenworth's estate because he is an "absent debtor", by order of Egbert Ten Eyck.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains agreement between Augustus Sacket, Peter Kembel, and Ezra Hounsfield.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes transfer of power of attorney from Peter Kemble and Ezra Hounsfield to Elisha Camp; granting power of attorney to Camp from Gerardus Camp and Enos Camp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes document granting Camp power of attorney Herman LeRoy, William Bayard, James MCEvers, John Wilkes, Louis LeGuen, Mallett Prevost; document giving Camp power of attorney for James De Le Ray de Chaumont.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes document granting Camp Power of Attorney from Cornelius Ray, William Bayard, Michael Hogan; petition to the Commissioners of Highways of the town of Hounsfield signed by twelve landholders who consider a road to Watertown necessary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes document granting Camp Power of Attorney from Enos Camp, Martha Camp, Augustus Sacket and wife Minerva, and Gerardus Camp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles of agreement between Augustus Sacket and Alexandre Biggam selling land; indenture leasing land to Nathan Miller and David Pierce by Samuel Hooker; land sale between Thomas Ogden and Camp; lease between Cornelius Ray, William Bayard and Michael Hogan and Samuel Hober; and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes list of subscribers betting on Stephen Van Renselaer's election as governor; rental agreement; document granting Camp Power of Attorney from Thomas Knox and Hester Gouverneur; document granting Camp Power of Attorney from Henry Champion and Lemuel Stores; John Coffin vs. Enoch Ely and Keziah, and Elisha Camp; indentures;

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indentures, leases, agreements. Includes handwritten census of 1814, town of Jefferson County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes agreement between Harriet Fulton and Robert and Edward Livingston giving the latter the right of steamboat navigation on Lake Ontario; bail plea from John Kirby;

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles of association of the Sackets Harbor Steam and Ferry Boat Company, Camp, Butterfiels, and William Kendall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition to divert a portion of the Black River into Pleasant Creek.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bill of complaint filed in case of James P. Allaire and others vs. Camp regarding steamboat profits, and compromise.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes record of proceedings in Canton for the election to fill vacancies in the 21st Regiment of the 5th Brigade of the New York Artillery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition to New York State Legistlature for additional surplus water tax.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter of patent for brick moulding machine by Ezra Fisk and Benjamin Hinkley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes several court oaths of citizenship.

did.unittitle: Indenture with attached map of French Creek and surrounding coastline
Series VI. Political documents, Speeches, Addresses, and Publications
Lists of voters, votes, and election outcomes
Tally of votes for New York Assembly
Political speech drafts and writings, no. 1-38
"History of the Steam Engine" by Elijah Galloway
Blank account book filled with various newspaper clippings
Blank account book filled with various newspaper clippings
